text:
Jacinta Allan has revealed her government will provide $1.5 million for Olympic and Paralympic teams ahead of the Paris 2024 games after the opposition called out Labor's "disgrace".

Premier Jacinta Allan has confirmed the Victorian government will provide a financial contribution to support Australians heading off to compete in the Paris games.

Ms Allan revealed to reporters on Thursday the government would chip in $1.5 million to be shared equally between Olympic and Paralympic athletes at this year's games.

The news came amid concerns being raised about the government giving athletes the "run-around" after being asked for funding in October and again in December.

Ms Allan said the financial support was only confirmed on Wednesday night at a budget committee meeting.

"I'm really pleased this morning to be able to confirm that the Victorian government will make a $1.5 million contribution split equally between our Olympic and Paralympic teams who are heading off to Paris," she said.

"We wish our athletes all the very best as they go and represent not just Victoria but indeed the nation… and we're really proud to be able to provide this support."

But shadow tourism, sport and events minister Sam Groth blasted the government for taking so long to confirm the funding, after previously axing the 2026 Commonwealth Games planned for regional Victoria.

"It's a disgrace that Labor could find $600 million to cancel the Commonwealth games, but gave Olympic and Paralympic athletes the run-around for months over just $1.5 million," Mr Groth said.

"The Liberals and Nationals support this funding commitment, but it never should've been this difficult."

New South Wales, Queensland, Western Australia and the Australian Capital Territory have reportedly already made contributions to the athletes.

Ms Allan's announcement came the same day as Australian Olympic Committee revealed the delegation and competition uniforms for Paris 2024.

"Every piece of the 2024 Olympic uniform features iconic Australian green and gold colour schemes and incorporates an Indigenous print, making this the largest integration of Indigenous artwork on athlete uniforms in Australian Olympic history," an AOC statement said.

The Olympic Games games will be held between July 26 and August 11, and the Paralympics will take place between August 28 and September 8.

title: Victorian government to chip in $1.5 million for Olympic and Paralympic athletes for Paris 2024 games | Sky News Australia
description: Premier Jacinta Allan has confirmed the Victorian government will provide a financial contribution to support Australians heading off to compete in the Paris games.
